this page</button></div><div class="theme-doc-markdown markdown"><header><h1>diagram.json File Format</h1></header><p>Each simulation project contains a diagram.json file. This file defines the components
that will be used for the simulation, their properties, and the connections between the
components.</p>
<h2 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="file-structure">File structure<a href="#file-structure" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to heading" title="Direct link to heading" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<p>The diagram file is a JSON file with several sections. The basic file structure is as
follows:</p>

<p><code>"version"</code> is always 1, <code>"author"</code> is the name of the person who created the
file, and <code>"editor"</code> is the name of the application that was used to edit the
file ("wokwi").</p>
<p>In addition, you can add a <code>"serialMonitor"</code> section to <a href="/guides/serial-monitor#configuring-the-serial-monitor">configure the Serial Monitor</a>.</p>
<h2 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="parts">Parts<a href="#parts" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to heading" title="Direct link to heading" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<p>The <code>"parts"</code> section defines the list of components in the simulation.
It's an array of objects with the following properties:</p>
<table><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>id</td><td>string</td><td>the unique identifier of the part (e.g. "led1")</td></tr><tr><td>type</td><td>string</td><td>the type of the part (e.g. "wokwi-led")</td></tr><tr><td>left</td><td>number</td><td>x screen coordinate (in pixels)</td></tr><tr><td>top</td><td>number</td><td>y screen coordinate (in pixels)</td></tr><tr><td>attrs</td><td>object</td><td>part attributes (e.g. "color" for wokwi-led)</td></tr><tr><td>rotate</td><td>number</td><td>rotation in degress (e.g. 90)</td></tr><tr><td>hide</td><td>boolean</td><td>if true, the part won't be visible</td></tr></tbody></table>
<p><code>id</code> and <code>type</code> are required, the other fields are optional.</p>
<p>For example, here's how you define a red LED called <code>"led1"</code> at position (x=100, y=50):</p>
<div class="language-json codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color: #bfc7d5; --prism-background-color: #292d3e;"><div class="codeBlockContent_QJqH"><pre tabindex="0" If two parts have the same "id",
the simulation may not function correctly.</p></div></div>
<div class="theme-admonition theme-admonition-tip admonition_xJq3 alert alert--success"><div class="admonitionHeading_Gvgb"><span class="admonitionIcon_Rf37"><svg viewBox="0 0 12 16"><path fill-rule="evenodd" d="M6.5 0C3.48 0 1 2.19 1 5c0 .92.55 2.25 1 3 1.34 2.25 1.78 2.78 2 4v1h5v-1c.22-1.22.66-1.75 2-4 .45-.75 1-2.08 1-3 0-2.81-2.48-5-5.5-5zm3.64 7.48c-.25.44-.47.8-.67 1.11-.86 1.41-1.25 2.06-1.45 3.23-.02.05-.02.11-.02.17H5c0-.06 0-.13-.02-.17-.2-1.17-.59-1.83-1.45-3.23-.2-.31-.42-.67-.67-1.11C2.44 6.78 2 5.65 2 5c0-2.2 2.02-4 4.5-4 1.22 0 2.36.42 3.22 1.19C10.55 2.94 11 3.94 11 5c0 .66-.44 1.78-.86 2.48zM4 14h5c-.23 1.14-1.3 2-2.5 2s-2.27-.86-2.5-2z"></path></svg></span>tip</div><div class="admonitionContent_BuS1"><p>Use <code>wokwi-cli lint</code> to validate your diagram.json file and catch common issues like unknown part types, invalid pin connections, and duplicate IDs. <p>A partial list of part types (e.g. <a href="/parts/wokwi-led">wokwi-led</a>) can be found under the "Diagram Reference" section of this guide. We're currently working to expand this list. Meanwhile, some of the parts are also documented at <a href="https://elements.wokwi.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Wokwi Elements</a>.</p>
<p>If your simulation project contains code, the diagram should include a microcontroller part that will execute your code. The following microcontrollers are currently supported:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="/parts/wokwi-attiny85"><code>wokwi-attiny85</code></a> - ATtiny85</li>

<li><a href="/parts/wokwi-arduino-uno"><code>wokwi-arduino-uno</code></a> - Arduino Uno R3</li>
<li><a href="/parts/wokwi-pi-pico"><code>wokwi-pi-pico</code></a> - Raspberry Pi Pico</li>
<li><code>board-esp32-devkit-c-v4</code> - ESP32 (official devkit)</li>
<li><code>wokwi-esp32-devkit-v1</code> - ESP32 (unofficial devkit)</li>
<li><code>board-esp32-c3-devkitm-1</code> - ESP32-C3</li>
<li><code>board-esp32-c3-rust-1</code> - ESP32-C3</li>
<li><code>board-esp32-c6-devkitc-1</code> - ESP32-C6</li>
<li><code>board-esp32-h2-devkitm-1</code> - ESP32-H2</li>
<li><code>board-esp32-s2-devkitm-1</code> - ESP32-S2</li>
<li><a href="/parts/board-franzininho-wifi"><code>board-franzininho-wifi</code></a> - ESP32-S2</li>
<li><code>board-esp32-s3-devkitc-1</code> - ESP32-S3</li>
<li><code>board-esp32-p4-preview</code> - ESP32-P4</li>
<li><a href="/parts/board-st-nucleo-c031c6"><code>board-st-nucleo-c031c6</code></a> - STM32 Nucleo-64 with STM32C031C6 MCU</li>
<li><a href="/parts/board-st-nucleo-l031k6"><code>board-st-nucleo-l031k6</code></a> - STM32 Nucleo-32 with STM32L031K6 MCU</li>
<li><code>board-xiao-esp32-c3</code> - ESP32-C3</li>
<li><code>board-xiao-esp32-c6</code> - ESP32-C6</li>

<div class="theme-admonition theme-admonition-tip admonition_xJq3 alert alert--success"><div class="admonitionHeading_Gvgb"><span class="admonitionIcon_Rf37"><svg viewBox="0 0 12 16"><path fill-rule="evenodd" d="M6.5 0C3.48 0 1 2.19 1 5c0 .92.55 2.25 1 3 1.34 2.25 1.78 2.78 2 4v1h5v-1c.22-1.22.66-1.75 2-4 .45-.75 1-2.08 1-3 0-2.81-2.48-5-5.5-5zm3.64 7.48c-.25.44-.47.8-.67 1.11-.86 1.41-1.25 2.06-1.45 3.23-.02.05-.02.11-.02.17H5c0-.06 0-.13-.02-.17-.2-1.17-.59-1.83-1.45-3.23-.2-.31-.42-.67-.67-1.11C2.44 6.78 2 5.65 2 5c0-2.2 2.02-4 4.5-4 1.22 0 2.36.42 3.22 1.19C10.55 2.94 11 3.94 11 5c0 .66-.44 1.78-.86 2.48zM4 14h5c-.23 1.14-1.3 2-2.5 2s-2.27-.86-2.5-2z"></path></svg></span>tip</div><div class="admonitionContent_BuS1"><p>Instead of manually specifying the left/top coordinates for each item, you

<h2 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="connections">Connections<a href="#connections" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to heading" title="Direct link to heading" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<p>The <code>"connections"</code> section defines how the parts are connected. Each connection is an array with four
items:</p>
<ul>
<li>The source component id and pin name, separated by a colon. e.g. <code>partId:pinName</code></li>
<li>The target component id and pin name</li>
<li>The color of the wire (or an empty string to hide the wire)</li>
<li>A list of instructions how to place the wire, as an array of strings (optional)</li>
</ul>
<p>For example, the following definition will connect the A (anode) pin of <code>led1</code>
to pin 13 of the <code>uno</code> part:</p>
<div class="language-json codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color: #bfc7d5; --prism-background-color: #292d3e;"><div class="codeBlockContent_QJqH"><pre tabindex="0" class="prism-code language-json codeBlock_bY9V thin-scrollbar" style="color: rgb(191, 199, 213); background-color: rgb(41, 45, 62);"><code class="codeBlockLines_e6Vv"><span class="token-line" style="color: rgb(191, 199, 213);"><span class="token plain">  </span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(199, 146, 234);">[</span><span class="token string" style="color: rgb(195, 232, 141);">"led1:A"</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(199, 146, 234);">,</span><span class="token plain"> </span><span class="token string" style="color: rgb(195, 232, 141);">"uno:13"</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(199, 146, 234);">,</span><span class="token plain"> </span><span class="token string" style="color: rgb(195, 232, 141);">"green"</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(199, 146, 234);">,</span><span class="token plain"> </span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(199, 146, 234);">[</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(199, 146, 234);">]</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(199, 146, 234);">]</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(199, 146, 234);">,</span><br></span></code></pre><div class="buttonGroup_M5ko"><button type="button" aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" title="Copy" class="clean-btn"><span class="copyButtonIcons_IEyt" aria-hidden="true"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonIcon_TrPX"><path fill="currentColor" d="M19,21H8V7H19M19,5H8A2,2 0 0,0 6,7V21A2,2 0 0,0 8,23H19A2,2 0 0,0 21,21V7A2,2 0 0,0 19,5M16,1H4A2,2 0 0,0 2,3V17H4V3H16V1Z"></path></svg><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonSuccessIcon_cVMy"><path fill="currentColor" d="M21,7L9,19L3.5,13.5L4.91,12.09L9,16.17L19.59,5.59L21,7Z"></path></svg></span></button></div></div></div>
<p>You can find the name of a component pin by moving the mouse over it.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="wire-placement-mini-language">Wire placement mini-language<a href="#wire-placement-mini-language" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to heading" title="Direct link to heading" translate="no">​</a></h3>
<p>Each item in the <code>"connections"</code> section can specify a list of instructions
how to draw the lines for the wire. Wires always go in straight lines, either
horizontally or vertically, and never diagonally.</p>
<p>There are three instructions:</p>
<ul>
<li>"v" followed by a number of pixels: move vertically (up/down)</li>
<li>"h" followed by a number of pixels: move horizontally (left/right)</li>
<li>"*" can appear only once. All the instructions that appear before the "*"
apply to the source pin, and the instructions that appear after it apply
to the target pins.</li>
</ul>
<p>For example:</p>
<div class="language-json codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color: #bfc7d5; --prism-background-color: #292d3e;"><div class="codeBlockContent_QJqH"><pre tabindex="0" class="prism-code language-json codeBlock_bY9V thin-scrollbar" style="color: rgb(191, 199, 213); background-color: rgb(41, 45, 62);"><code class="codeBlockLines_e6Vv"><span class="token-line" style="color: rgb(191, 199, 213);"><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(199, 146, 234);">[</span><span class="token string" style="color: rgb(195, 232, 141);">"v10"</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(199, 146, 234);">,</span><span class="token plain"> </span><span class="token string" style="color: rgb(195, 232, 141);">"h5"</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(199, 146, 234);">,</span><span class="token plain"> </span><span class="token string" style="color: rgb(195, 232, 141);">"*"</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(199, 146, 234);">,</span><span class="token plain"> </span><span class="token string" style="color: rgb(195, 232, 141);">"v-15"</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(199, 146, 234);">,</span><span class="token plain"> </span><span class="token string" style="color: rgb(195, 232, 141);">"h10"</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(199, 146, 234);">]</span><br></span></code></pre><div class="buttonGroup_M5ko"><button type="button" aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" title="Copy" class="clean-btn"><span class="copyButtonIcons_IEyt" aria-hidden="true"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonIcon_TrPX"><path fill="currentColor" d="M19,21H8V7H19M19,5H8A2,2 0 0,0 6,7V21A2,2 0 0,0 8,23H19A2,2 0 0,0 21,21V7A2,2 0 0,0 19,5M16,1H4A2,2 0 0,0 2,3V17H4V3H16V1Z"></path></svg><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonSuccessIcon_cVMy"><path fill="currentColor" d="M21,7L9,19L3.5,13.5L4.91,12.09L9,16.17L19.59,5.59L21,7Z"></path></svg></span></button></div></div></div>
<p>The "v10" will move 10 pixels down from the source pin, then "h5" will move
five pixels the right.</p>
<p>The instructions that appear after the "*" are applied in reverse order: "h10" will
move 10 pixels right of the target pin, then "v-15" will move 15 pixels up.</p>
<p>Finally, the simulator will connect the two ends of the wire with a combination
of horizontal and a vertical wire that cover the remaining distance, as necessary.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="wire-placement-animation">Wire placement animation<a href="#wire-placement-animation" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to heading" title="Direct link to heading" translate="no">​</a></h3>
